questions.question: What is the topic for Week 27?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Twenty-Seven is GET RICH QUICK SYNDROME.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 25?
questions.explanation: Strength in Quietness was the topic for Week 25.

questions.question: Wealth gained by dishonesty will be diminished: but he who gathers by ________ will increase.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Proverbs 13:11) says 'he who gathers by labour will increase.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is Proverbs 13:11.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 27 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: Wealth gained by dishonesty will be diminished: but he who gathers by labour will increase.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of Proverbs 13:11.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (Proverbs 13:11), what will happen to wealth gained by dishonesty?
questions.explanation: The verse says 'Wealth gained by dishonesty will be diminished...'

questions.question: According to the introduction, wealth and living a comfortable life are NOT part of God's plan for humanity.
questions.explanation: The text states 'Wealth and living a comfortable life are part of God’s plan for humanity.'

questions.question: According to the Week 27 lesson's introduction, what has driven many youths to adopt the get-rich-quick syndrome?
questions.explanation: The text says '...yet the lack of patience has driven many, especially youths, to adopt the harmful “get-rich-quick syndrome”...'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE SYNDROME'.

questions.question: How does Outline I describe the 'get-rich-quick syndrome'?
options: B. An intense and irrational desire to accumulate wealth rapidly, often without considering the consequences
C. Working three jobs at once
questions.explanation: The text says 'The get-rich-quick syndrome refers to an intense and irrational desire to accumulate wealth rapidly, often without considering the consequences.'

questions.question: Which of these is listed as a negative consequence of the get-rich-quick syndrome?
options: A. An increase in traditional values
C. A deterioration in educational standards and erosion of traditional values
questions.explanation: The text lists 'a deterioration in educational standards; the erosion of traditional values like honesty, hard work, and fairness...'

questions.question: According to the Week 27 lesson, a lack of genuine satisfaction often leads to sudden death as a consequence of this syndrome.
questions.explanation: The text says '...and lack of genuine satisfaction which often leads to sudden death (Proverbs 1:18-19).'

questions.question: Which of these is considered a 'red flag' for a get-rich-quick scam?
options: A. Being asked to study for a degree
B. An offer that takes years to bring profit
C. Being asked to use one's account for transfers, withdrawals, or other financial transactions
D. Being asked to volunteer at a charity
questions.explanation: The text says '...and being asked to use one’s account for transfers, withdrawals, or other financial transactions (Proverbs 1:10).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'RESIST THE URGE'.

questions.question: How can an individual overcome the urge for quick wealth syndrome?
options: A. By moving to another country
B. By trusting God to empower and teach you to make wealth
C. By avoiding all business ventures
D. By trusting in their own cleverness
questions.explanation: Point in Outline II says 'you can overcome the urge for quick wealth syndrome by trusting God to empower and teach you to make wealth...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that God empowers and teaches you to make wealth?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Deuteronomy 8:18; Isaiah 48:17)'.

questions.question: According to the Week 27 lesson, how should society shift the spotlight?
options: D. From local to international news
questions.explanation: The text says '...and shifting the spotlight from wealth to integrity by ceasing to glorify individuals with questionable characters flaunting wealth...'

questions.question: According to the Week 27 lesson, creating job opportunities and empowering youths through skill acquisition are crucial steps society can take.
questions.explanation: The text says 'Additionally, creating job opportunities, empowering youths through skill acquisition, and supporting entrepreneurship are crucial steps...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support staying diligent and dedicated to your goals?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Stay diligent and dedicated to your goals (Proverbs 22:29)...'

questions.question: The conclusion warns that the get-rich-quick syndrome is a plan to make a lot of money in a short time, often not minding the risk.
questions.explanation: The conclusion states: 'The get-rich-quick syndrome is a plan to make a lot of money in a short time, often with little effort and not minding the risk, beware (1 Timothy 6:10).'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Blessed is the man that trusteth in the LORD, and whose hope the LORD is.'
questions.verse_text: Blessed is the man that trusteth in the LORD, and whose hope the LORD is.
questions.explanation: This is verse 7 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Jeremiah 17:8), the man who trusts in the Lord shall be like what?
options: A. A lion in the forest
B. A tree planted by the waters
C. A house built on a rock
questions.explanation: Verse 8 says 'For he shall be as a tree planted by the waters...'

questions.question: According to Jeremiah 17:9, what is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked?
questions.explanation: Verse 9 says 'The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked...'

questions.question: According to Jeremiah 17:10, what does the Lord do to give every man according to his ways?
options: A. He searches the heart and tries the reins
B. He opens the books of heaven
C. He asks the angels for a report
questions.explanation: Verse 10 says 'I the LORD search the heart, I try the reins, even to give every man according to his ways...'

questions.question: In Jeremiah 17:11, he that gets riches, and not by right, is compared to what bird?
questions.explanation: Verse 11 says 'As the partridge sitteth on eggs, and hatcheth them not...'

questions.question: According to Jeremiah 17:11, what will happen to the man who gets riches not by right at his end?
options: A. He shall be a king
B. He shall be a fool
questions.explanation: Verse 11 concludes '...shall leave them in the midst of his days, and at his end shall be a fool.'

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited in Outline II to support seeking wisdom, practicing patience, and cultivating contentment?
questions.explanation: The text lists '(Ecclesiastes 7:12; Hebrews 13:5; 1 Timothy 6:6)' at the end of the first paragraph of Outline II.

questions.question: One of the red flags for a scam is a stranger who claims to be connected through mutual acquaintances and proposes a business opportunity.
questions.explanation: The text specifically mentions this as a red flag in Outline I.

questions.question: Efforts should focus on promoting value-based orientation, emphasising that the ________ matter as much as the ends.
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'emphasising that the means matter as much as the ends...'

questions.question: What is one way society can combat this syndrome regarding its exposure channels?
options: A. By shutting down the internet
B. By educating individuals on its channels of exposure, such as social media
C. By making all news illegal
D. By paying everyone a high salary
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'Society can play a vital role in combating this syndrome by educating individuals on its channels of exposure—such as social media...'
